terms.definition: The parent public corpus/site spine that holds reviewed editions, artifacts, lexicon entries, and provenance packets.
A LATENS concept lane reserved for FERROGLYPH editions and artifacts.
A LATENS concept lane reserved for OZVENA editions and artifacts.
The required metadata chain that lets a public artifact be traced to its model, prompt seed, run, sources, approvals, and redaction boundary.
A reviewed descriptive tag set for the artifact's intended affective profile; it is not a psychological diagnosis or user inference.
The stable, redacted prompt descriptor used to reproduce or audit the generation without exposing private notes or credentials.
The durable identifier for one generation or editorial run.
The durable identifier for an approved release grouping one or more public artifacts.
The durable identifier for one public artifact within an edition.
The reviewed references that support public context or claims. Private notes are represented by redacted descriptors only.
The documented human review step required before public publication, push, merge, deploy, or production dispatch.
The rule that public pages may expose only sanitized metadata while private review packets may contain restricted operational context.
